title: .containerignore files in additional build contexts are ignored
body:
### Issue Description

When building images using multiple contexts (--build-context) there is no way to use .containerignore files to skip certain files from being COPYed.

Neither placing the .containerignore file in the main context nor in the additional context works.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman build` using `--build-context=the_additional_context=somedir` to add a secondary build context.
2. Have some content in `somedir` directory, i.e. a Git repo with its `.git` directory.
3. Create `somedir/.containerignore` file with `**/.git` in order not to add it to the final image (or even **/*, it doesn't matter).
4. In Containerfile, `COPY` some content `--from=the_additional_context`.
5. Build and run a shell in the container and observe the COPYed directory, the .containerignore patterns were not honored.


### Describe the results you received

.containerignore patterns are ignored in respect of additional build contexts

### Describe the results you expected

.containerignore patterns are honored and ignored files are not COPYed

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL 8 VM on RHV

### Additional information

.containerignore works properly when referring files coming from the main context, not from additional ones.
